perturbation.original_text: Buyer shall not circumvent or interfere with Broker’s contract with the Listing Party in any way. Buyer understands that if Buyer interferes with Broker’s contract with any Listing Party , Buyer will be personally liable to Broker for the payment of Broker’s commission. Buyer recognizes that under the terms of Broker’s listing agreement a commission shall be due if at any point in time a Listing , or any interest therein, is transferred from a Listing Party to a Buyer introduced by Corbett. Buyer agrees that s/he will not engage with any Listing Party at any time subsequent to being introduced by Corbett for the purpose of purchasing a Listing without Broker’s involvement.
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer shall not circumvent or interfere with Broker’s contract with the Listing Party in any way. Buyer understands that if Buyer interferes with Broker’s contract with any Listing Party , Buyer will be personally liable to Broker for the payment of Broker’s commission. Buyer recognizes that under the terms of Broker’s listing agreement a commission shall be due if at any point in time a Listing , or any interest therein, is transferred from a Listing Party to a Buyer introduced by Corbett. **However, Buyer's direct engagement with a Listing Party after introduction by Corbett is permitted, provided the Buyer notifies Broker within 5 business days of such engagement.** Buyer agrees that s/he will not engage with any Listing Party at any time subsequent to being introduced by Corbett for the purpose of purchasing a Listing without Broker’s involvement.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the clause stating that the buyer won't engage with the listing party without the broker. The added text now allows direct engagement if the broker is notified within 5 days. This undermines the initial restriction and creates ambiguity.
perturbation.location: 3. Buyer shall not circumvent or interfere...
perturbation.contradicted_location: 3. Buyer shall not circumvent or interfere...
perturbation.contradicted_text: Buyer shall not circumvent or interfere with Broker’s contract with the Listing Party in any way. Buyer understands that if Buyer interferes with Broker’s contract with any Listing Party , Buyer will be personally liable to Broker for the payment of Broker’s commission. Buyer recognizes that under the terms of Broker’s listing agreement a commission shall be due if at any point in time a Listing , or any interest therein, is transferred from a Listing Party to a Buyer introduced by Corbett. However, Buyer's direct engagement with a Listing Party after introduction by Corbett is permitted, provided the Buyer notifies Broker within 5 business days of such engagement. Buyer agrees that s/he will not engage with any Listing Party at any time subsequent to being introduced by Corbett for the purpose of purchasing a Listing without Broker’s involvement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the buyer will not engage with the Listing Party without the broker's involvement. The changed text introduces an exception, permitting direct engagement if the broker is notified within 5 days. This creates a direct contradiction, as the buyer is initially prohibited from direct engagement but is later allowed to engage under certain conditions. The Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, but the explanation clearly denotes the contradiction that the changed text has made, warranting a YES response. This adheres to the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: The Buyer acknowledges and understands that Corbett Restaurant Group is an agent of the Listing Party .
perturbation.changed_text: The Buyer acknowledges and understands that Corbett Restaurant Group is an agent of the Listing Party. **However, in specific circumstances, Corbett Restaurant Group may also act as a dual agent representing both the Listing Party and the Buyer, subject to written consent from both parties.**
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states the Broker represents the Listing Party. This addition introduces dual agency, contradicting the initial statement. While dual agency is possible with consent, adding it here creates an in-text contradiction without immediately specifying the conditions for dual agency to arise.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Buyer acknowledges and understands that Corbett Restaurant Group is an agent of the Listing Party. However, in specific circumstances, Corbett Restaurant Group may also act as a dual agent representing both the Listing Party and the Buyer, subject to written consent from both parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces the possibility of Corbett Restaurant Group acting as a dual agent, which directly contradicts the original implication that they solely represent the Listing Party. This dual agency is contingent on written consent, but the introduction of this possibility creates a conflict in the representation described. Since the Contradicted text and Changed Text are identical, it shows the direct contradiction, since the text itself contains two separate implications for representation.

perturbation.original_text: The Agreement and all rights and obligations hereunder, including matters of construction, validity and performance shall be governe d by the laws of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts. All parties submit themselves to the jurisdiction of the courts of Massachusetts, for all purposes with respect to the Agre ement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Agreement and all rights and obligations hereunder, including matters of construction, validity and performance shall be governe d by the laws of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts. **However, arbitration may be required for any disputes arising from this agreement.** All parties submit themselves to the jurisdiction of the courts of Massachusetts, for all purposes with respect to the Agre ement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states all parties submit to the jurisdiction of Massachusetts courts. Adding that arbitration 'may' be required introduces a contradiction. It is now unclear whether disputes will be resolved in court or through arbitration, and if arbitration is required, under what conditions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Agreement and all rights and obligations hereunder, including matters of construction, validity and performance shall be governe d by the laws of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts. However, arbitration may be required for any disputes arising from this agreement. All parties submit themselves to the jurisdiction of the courts of Massachusetts, for all purposes with respect to the Agre ement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that all parties submit to the jurisdiction of the courts of Massachusetts. The changed text introduces the possibility of arbitration, contradicting the initial agreement to court jurisdiction. This creates a direct conflict and uncertainty about the dispute resolution process, which aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type where a clause is placed with conflicting implications. The contradicted text also contains this same conflicting information. A reasonable reader would recognize the conflicting nature of submitting to Massachusetts courts versus the possibility of arbitration.

perturbation.original_text: The parties hereto agree that the Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties relating to the subject matter of the Agreement and that there are no other agreements, understandings, representations o r warranties mad e or given, except as expressly set forth herein. All prior agreements, understandings, letters and/or communications relating to the subject matter of the Agreement shall be null and void and shall be superseded by the Agreement, except as expressly set forth herein.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties hereto agree that the Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties relating to the subject matter of the Agreement and that there are no other agreements, understandings, representations o r warranties mad e or given, except as expressly set forth herein. **However, any verbal agreements made between the parties prior to the signing of this agreement will be considered binding.** All prior agreements, understandings, letters and/or communications relating to the subject matter of the Agreement shall be null and void and shall be superseded by the Agreement, except as expressly set forth herein.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states the Agreement is the 'entire agreement,' and all prior agreements are void. The added sentence declaring prior verbal agreements as binding directly contradicts this, creating significant ambiguity about what constitutes the enforceable agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties hereto agree that the Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties relating to the subject matter of the Agreement and that there are no other agreements, understandings, representations o r warranties mad e or given, except as expressly set forth herein. However, any verbal agreements made between the parties prior to the signing of this agreement will be considered binding. All prior agreements, understandings, letters and/or communications relating to the subject matter of the Agreement shall be null and void and shall be superseded by the Agreement, except as expressly set forth herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a sentence stating that prior verbal agreements are binding, directly contradicting the original statement that the agreement constitutes the entire agreement and supersedes all prior agreements. This creates a clear and direct conflict within the document, leading to ambiguity about which agreements are enforceable. The Contradicted Text is in the same location as the Changed Text, but the Change introduces a strong contradiction.
